title: Dharus

{{Quote|Heave to, you Imperial garbage scow! Prepare to be boarded!|Dharus|<ref name="GG9" />}}
'''Dharus''' was a [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] associated with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. A member of a [[Dharus' species|little-known species]], he was provided with information about [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] activity and prize [[Starship/Legends|starships]] in the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] called [[Leisure Corridor]], and in exchange he shared his profits with the Alliance and took [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] for them in a mutually profitable agreement—even if the [[Alliance observer]]s assigned to Dharus were aghast at the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate's]] activities. Dharus' raids were embarrassing to the Galactic Empire, which offered a high [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] for him. A [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] managed to capture Dharus but, as he was a valuable partner of the Alliance, a Rebel team rescued him. Dharus then returned to his pirate activities, increasing his area of influence as well as the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] under his command.

He was also known to post in illegal publications [[Cynabar's Droid Datalog]] and [[Gundark's Gear Datalog]] about his favorite technologies. During the [[Caamas Document Crisis]], Dharus, by then a retired [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], returned to active duty as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Officer/Legends|leader]] and strategist for the [[Nhoras]], charging a hefty sum for his services.

==Biography==
===Rebel privateer===
{{Quote|Malvarra, I must refuse your request to transfer from your current post observing the privateer Dharus. Your tour is not yet one-third over, and your reasons for requesting transfer are inadequate. Never fear: they treated their first three observers much, ''much'' worse. You are in excellent company in requesting your transfer—nearly every other observer assigned had made the same request. However, none of Dharus' antics have proven fatal yet, so relax and remind yourself of your duty.|Ral'Rai Muvunc in a message to Moris Malvarra|<ref name="PP" />}}
Dharus was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of a [[Dharus' species|little-known sentient species]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], he was active as a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]]. Before [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], he became a [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] at a point where the Alliance was ready to ally with independent pirates in a mutually beneficial agreement. Dharus was given a [[Letter of marque/Legends|letter of marque]] specifying the terms of the arrangement, and following Alliance protocol, he was assigned an [[Alliance observer]] to be stationed on board his [[Starship/Legends|starship]] serving as his contact. At the same time, the observer ensured that Dharus would not break the settlement. Following the terms of the letter, Dharus was not a member of the Alliance but an independent partner, and as such the observer could not have given orders to Dharus or to any member of his crew.<ref name="PP" />

Dharus and his crew treated their [[Dharus' first observer|assigned observer]] poorly, causing the observer to ask [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] for a transfer to a different post. A similar situation happened with the [[Dharus' second observer|replacement observer]], and with the [[Dharus' third observer|third observer]] assigned to Dharus. Noticing the situation, the Alliance repeatedly investigated Dharus' activities, but they found no reason to revoke his letter of marque. Dharus' fourth observer, [[Moris Malvarra]], also asked for a transfer before one-third of his assigned tour [[Time/Legends|time]] had passed. Alliance [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Ral'Rai Muvunc]] rejected Malvarra's request, explaining that not enough reasons had been provided; Muvunc also assured Malvarra that he would not be considered responsible for Dharus' so-called atrocious actions.<ref name="PP" />

Dharus performed his raids in [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]], particularly in a trade and tourism [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] known as the [[Leisure Corridor]], which went from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] to [[Ebiwaan]],<ref name="PP" /><ref name="GG9" /> in the [[Sarin sector/Legends|Sarin sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The area had a strong Imperial presence, including heavy patrol ships. Dharus depended on intelligence provided by the Alliance to choose his targets and avoid traps. In exchange for such a valuable asset, Dharus agreed not only to attack Alliance-approved targets, but also to take [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] and hostages who were important for the Alliance. Dharus was also known to capture, or to pretend to capture, certain Imperial citizens who wanted to desert.<ref name="GG9" /> As a [[Rebel privateer]], Dharus shared the profits of his raids with the Alliance, following the terms of the letter of marque.<ref name="PP" /><ref name="GG9" />

===Capture and release===
Dharus became a well-known privateer, infamous in certain circles. The Empire offered a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 40,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for his head,<ref name="PP" /><ref name="GG9" /> accusing him of having performed several raids on their ships.<ref name="PP" /> The Alliance also used Dharus for [[Propaganda/Legends|propaganda]] purposes, embarrassing the Empire with his activities. Circa 0 BBY, the independent [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Zardra]] went after Dharus' bounty. Zardra was on board the ''[[Culroon Minstrel]]'', a [[Jesoni-class liner|''Jesoni''-class liner]] that could easily be a "prize" for Dharus. The Alliance failed to detect Zardra because she was not an Imperial agent and looked like an Imperial [[Noble/Legends|noble]]. Dharus and his crew fell into Zardra's trap and boarded the ''Minstrel'', and the bounty hunter defeated the crew and captured Dharus.<ref name="GG9" /> She then took her prisoner to the Empire in exchange for the reward.<ref name="PP" /> The fight between Zardra and Dharus gave the ''Minstrel'' some fame, and it became a [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] of choice for thrill-seeking dilettantes.<ref name="GG9" />

As Dharus was an important asset to the Alliance, a Rebel team conducted a secret operation to rescue the privateer. The Empire was unprepared for their movement, and Dharus was freed six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after his capture, before his own trial was able to even be held.<ref name="GG9" /> Embarrassed, the Empire increased the bounty for Dharus to 90,000 credits.<ref name="PP" />

Dharus began operating in the [[Parmel sector/Legends|Parmel]], [[Sarin sector/Legends|Sarin]],<ref name="GG9" /> and [[Portmoak sector/Legends|Portmoak sectors]],<ref name="CFT">''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]''</ref> without forfeiting his initial mobile headquarters in the Leisure Corridor.<ref name="PP" /> Initially, the privateer in Parmel and Sarin was believed not to be Dharus, but a copycat.<ref name="GG9" />

===Dharus' Buccaneers===
[[File:Dharus_by_Marshall_Andrews_from_Pirates_and_Privateers.jpg|thumb|left|Dharus recruiting some buccaneers.]]
At that point, Dharus organized a group of ninety-six [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] under his command,<ref name="PP" /><ref name="GG9" /> officially named [[Dharus' Buccaneers]].<ref name="PP" /> He led a three-ship [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] formed by two heavily-modified [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub]] [[Corsair-class cruiser|''Corsair''-class cruisers]], the ''[[Red Lancer]]'' and the ''[[Blue Queen]]''; and a [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|CEC]] [[DP20 gunship/Legends|DP20 frigate]], the ''[[Dark Revenge]]'',<ref name="PP" /><ref name="GG9" /> with several illegal customizations.<ref name="PP" /> The ''Red Lancer'' and the ''Blue Queen'' had increased weaponry, but Dharus was forced to sacrifice cargo space, speed, crew comfort, and security measures in the [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]]—although he was trying to design custom [[hyperdrive sled]]s to solve this last problem.<ref name="GG9" />

Each of the cruisers was operated by a crew of two plus one [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]]; the [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] used forty-five crewmen plus forty-six gunners. In a short time, Dharus used his command skills to create a loyal, multi-ship crew that operated as a team. The cruisers generally waited at a [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] exit point and threatened any target ship appearing there. If the ship jettisoned its cargo, the privateers picked it up; otherwise, the cruiser used [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] to disable the ship, and then the crew of the frigate would break the [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]] and board it.<ref name="GG9" /> Although relentless, Dharus was not unnecessarily aggressive and had a low violence rate for a pirate.<ref name="PP" />

Dharus was known to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] against swarms of Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]]. He also defeated an Imperial [[Star Galleon-class frigate|''Star Galleon''-class frigate]] before [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]. At that point, his cruisers were seen in the [[Quence sector/Legends|Quence sector]], near [[Elshandruu Pica]].<ref name="GG9" /> With this new array of activity, the Empire added to the charges against Dharus and, besides piracy and theft, accused him of [[Treason/Legends|treason]], conspiracy, escape and flight, aggression, and also of illegal operation of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]].<ref name="PP" />

===Further activities===
Being an active criminal, Dharus posted in [[Cynabar's Droid Datalog]] about his predilection for [[Medtech Industries/Legends|Medtech]] [[FX-series medical assistant droid/Legends|FX medical droids]]. A person named [[Cormun (Cynabar's Droid Datalog)|Cormun]] identified Dharus and replied to his post by specifying that Dharus was a pirate, the [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] where he was active, and the venture [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] could have by selling him expensive parts for his FX [[Droid/Legends|droids]].<ref name="CFT" />

At a later date, Cormun' posted about the [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]] [[CLL-8 binary load lifter]] droid on the same datalog, expressing his predilection for the combat modification package for that droid so that it could fight customs security forces. After reading that post at Elshandruu Pica, Dharus replied that a CLL-8 required high-damage weapons to stop the armored Imperial customs troops, but at the same time the droid was unintelligent enough to damage the hull of the ship while firing, killing everyone in the area. The pirate, thus, believed that arming such a droid was a ridiculous idea.<ref name="AJ14">{{JournalCite|14|Cynabar's Droid Datalog (article series)}}</ref>

At some point before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], while at Elshandruu Pica, Dharus won the [[GX1 short hauler/Legends|Short Hauler]] ''[[Nova Whisper]]'' from a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] commodities shipper by the name of [[Tev Driscull]]. Dharus then improved the weaponry of the ship and added [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] [[Cloaking device/Legends|stealth devices]]. A few months later, the ''Nova Whisper'' was captured by agents of the [[Corellian Security Force]].<ref name="ss">''[[Stock Ships]]''</ref>

After this event, Dharus eventually retired. In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], however, he was asked to return by the [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Nhoras]]. The Nhora had had a centuries-lasting conflict with another species, the [[Clatear]]s; as of then, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government was too busy with other priorities to interfere, so the Nhoras and the Clatears had decided to fight again. As the Nhoras were less militaristic than the Clatears at that stage, they decided to hire [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] and offered Dharus the high sum of 30,000 credits per day to [[Officer/Legends|lead]] their [[Army/Legends|army]]. Dharus accepted and led the [[Dhashaan Shield]] mercenary group protecting the Nhora [[Star system/Legends|systems]]; he also took charge of the Nhoras' logistics and strategy.<ref name="SotP" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|There's nothing quite as satisfying as slicing through the airlock of a prize vessel with ease. I always keep a fusion cutter in my ship's store.|Dharus|<ref name="GFT">''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Dharus_Buccaneers.svg|thumb|left|Dharus' insignia]]
Dharus was a member of an [[Humanoid/Legends|anthropomorphic]] species<ref name="PP" /><ref name="GG9" /> with no visible [[Ear/Legends|ears]] and a flat nose. He also had a ridge on his brow. He had bright [[red]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], [[orange]]-[[brown]] skin, and long brown hair that he tied in a twin braid behind and in front of his neck; he called this a "[[ceremonial braid]]." Dharus favored bright tunics of several different [[Color/Legends|colors]], although his pants were always [[black]]. He also wore short utility [[Boot/Legends|boots]].<ref name="GG9" />

A tall sentient in excellent physical shape, Dharus was unruly, clamorous, jolly and a bon-vivant. He had never liked the Empire, and his opinion of them grew worse after his capture. He decided to sell his services to the Alliance so that he then could not only raid Imperial ships but also mock his victims.<ref name="GG9" />

Known as a good fighter either with his own fists or [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], Dharus was never seen without his weapons of choice: a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]], a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]], and a [[Caliban (manufacturer)/Legends|Caliban]] [[Model X/Legends|Model X]] [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]]. He also carried [[Comlink/Legends|comlinks]], [[Datapad/Legends|datapads]], and [[Medpac/Legends|medpacs]].<ref name="GG9" /> A great fan of the [[Borallis Metalworking & Materials]] [[PCW-880]] [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusioncutter]], he enjoyed using this tool to cut the airlocks of ships he wanted to board.<ref name="GFT" /> He also liked the FX-series of [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droids]], particularly the [[FX-7 medical assistant droid/Legends|FX-7 medical assistant droid]], even though it had been superseded by more recent and efficient models.<ref name="CFT" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dharus was first mentioned in ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'' ([[1993]]), a supplement to ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', published by [[West End Games]] and written by [[Simon Smith]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann|Eric Trautmann]]. The book included an image of Dharus drawn by [[Mike Vilardi]]. In [[1997]], Dharus was mentioned in four more [[Roleplaying game|role-playing]] books by West End Games, and also in ''[[Specter of the Past]]'', a novel written by [[Timothy Zahn]]. [[Scum and Villainy (sourcebook)|''Scum and Villainy'']], a [[2008]] sourcebook for the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook|Saga Edition]] of the Roleplaying Game, lists Dharus among the example characters of the "master privateer" class.

Dharus' species is specifically defined as "unknown" in both ''Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim'' and ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]''. Following his RPG stats, Dharus is a great pilot specializing in [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]]. He has a commanding presence over his crew, and can intimidate people not under his command. He is also a good negotiator and [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]], and is knowledgeable about [[Star/Legends|star]] systems and [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species. Although Dharus tried to design hyperdrive sleds for his crew, his abilities in technology are low.<ref name="GG9" />
